title: New Drug Offers Hope for Patients with Pancreatic Cancer
subtitle: A daily pill shows promising results in clinical trials, potentially changing how doctors treat the world's deadliest cancer.

summary.en: A new drug called daraxonrasib has shown the ability to double survival time for patients with advanced pancreatic cancer. The drug works by blocking a protein that helps cancer cells grow and spread. Clinical trial results presented at a major cancer conference were described by experts as a significant breakthrough. Unlike traditional chemotherapy, the pill has fewer and less severe side effects for patients. While full approval is still pending, the FDA has already allowed expanded access to the treatment.
summary.zh: 一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥已被證實能讓晚期胰臟癌患者的存活時間延長一倍。該藥物透過阻斷一種協助癌細胞生長與擴散的蛋白質來發揮作用。在大型癌症會議上發表的臨床試驗結果，被專家譽為重大突破。與傳統化學治療不同，這種口服藥物對患者造成的副作用較少且較輕微。儘管尚未獲得全面核准，但美國食品藥物管理局已允許擴大該療法的取得管道。

paragraphs.en: Pancreatic cancer has long been considered one of the most difficult diseases to treat. For many years, patients have had very few options, and survival rates have remained low. However, researchers recently announced a major breakthrough that could change the future of care for this illness. A new daily pill, known as daraxonrasib, has shown the ability to double the survival time for patients whose cancer has already spread.
paragraphs.zh: 胰臟癌長期以來被認為是最難治療的疾病之一。多年來，患者的選擇非常有限，存活率也一直處於低點。然而，研究人員近期宣布了一項重大突破，可能改變這種疾病的未來照護方式。一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新型每日口服藥物，已被證實能讓癌症已經擴散的患者存活時間延長一倍。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, the results of this clinical trial were presented at the annual meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) in Chicago. Experts at the conference described the findings as a "gamechanger" and one of the most important developments in the field in decades. Dr. Rachna Shroff, an oncology expert at the University of Arizona Cancer Center, noted that the results were "landscape-changing" and that doctors are now seeing "unprecedented survival" rates for patients.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，這項臨床試驗的結果是在芝加哥舉行的美國臨床腫瘤學會（ASCO）年會上發表的。與會專家將此發現形容為「改變遊戲規則」的成果，也是該領域數十年來最重要的發展之一。亞利桑那大學癌症中心（University of Arizona Cancer Center）的腫瘤學專家拉赫娜·史洛夫（Rachna Shroff）博士指出，這些結果「改變了治療版圖」，醫師們現在正見證患者出現「前所未有的存活率」。

paragraphs.en: To understand why this is important, it helps to look at how the disease works. Pancreatic cancer is often found late, meaning it has already spread to other parts of the body by the time of diagnosis. In the trial of 500 patients, those who took daraxonrasib lived for an average of 13.2 months. In comparison, patients who received standard chemotherapy lived for only about 6.6 to 6.7 months. MedPage Today reported that the study was published in the New England Journal of Medicine, confirming that the drug significantly improved both overall survival and the time patients lived without their cancer getting worse.
paragraphs.zh: 要理解為何這項發現如此重要，必須先了解該疾病的運作機制。胰臟癌通常發現得較晚，這意味著在確診時，癌細胞往往已經擴散到身體的其他部位。在這項包含五百名患者的試驗中，服用 daraxonrasib 的患者平均存活了十三點二個月。相較之下，接受標準化學治療的患者平均僅存活約六點六至六點七個月。《今日醫學報導》（MedPage Today）指出，這項研究發表於《新英格蘭醫學期刊》（New England Journal of Medicine），證實該藥物顯著改善了患者的整體存活率，並延長了癌症未惡化的時間。

paragraphs.en: The drug works by targeting a specific protein called Kras. This protein is found in nearly all pancreatic cancers and acts like a fuel that tells cancer cells to grow and divide. By "gluing" molecules together, the drug shuts down the Kras protein, effectively stopping the cancer's growth. Dr. Brian Wolpin of the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, who led the trial, explained that this new medicine targets the molecular basis of the disease. He believes this will make the cancer much more treatable in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 這種藥物的作用機制是鎖定一種稱為 Kras 的特定蛋白質。這種蛋白質存在於幾乎所有的胰臟癌中，就像燃料一樣，指揮癌細胞生長與分裂。透過將分子「黏合」在一起，該藥物能阻斷 Kras 蛋白質，進而有效停止癌症的生長。領導這項試驗的丹娜法伯癌症研究所（Dana-Farber Cancer Institute）布萊恩·沃爾平（Brian Wolpin）博士解釋，這種新藥針對的是疾病的分子基礎。他相信這將使胰臟癌在未來變得更容易治療。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the survival benefits, the quality of life for patients is a major factor. NPR reports that chemotherapy is often very difficult for patients to tolerate, leading many to stop treatment because of harsh side effects. In contrast, daraxonrasib causes fewer and less severe problems. While some patients may experience an acne-like rash, it is generally much easier to manage than the effects of traditional chemotherapy. This is important because it allows patients to spend their extra time feeling better.
paragraphs.zh: 除了存活率的提升，患者的生活品質也是一項重要考量。美國公共廣播電台（NPR）報導指出，化學治療對患者而言通常難以忍受，導致許多人因為嚴重的副作用而被迫停止治療。相比之下，daraxonrasib 引起的副作用較少且較輕微。雖然部分患者可能會出現類似青春痘的紅疹，但通常比傳統化學治療的副作用更容易處理。這一點至關重要，因為它讓患者能在多出來的存活時間裡，感覺更舒適。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Julie Gralow, the chief medical officer at ASCO, described the study as a "grand slam" for cancer research. She noted that while other drugs have successfully targeted similar mutations in lung and colorectal cancers, this is the first time such a success has been seen in pancreatic cancer. The medical community is hopeful that this "Ras revolution," as some experts call it, will lead to even more effective treatments in the coming years.
paragraphs.zh: 美國臨床腫瘤學會的醫療長茱莉·格拉洛（Julie Gralow）博士將這項研究形容為癌症研究的「滿貫全壘打」。她指出，雖然其他藥物已成功鎖定肺癌與大腸直腸癌中類似的突變，但這是首次在胰臟癌中見到如此成功的案例。醫學界對於這場被部分專家稱為「Ras 革命」的進展感到樂觀，並期待未來幾年能出現更有效的治療方法。

paragraphs.en: What comes next for patients? While the drug has not yet received full approval from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the agency has already taken steps to make it more available. According to NPR, the FDA has expanded access to the drug, allowing doctors to provide it to patients even before the final approval process is complete. This is a positive sign for those who have been waiting for new, effective options.
paragraphs.zh: 接下來患者該怎麼辦？雖然該藥物尚未獲得美國食品藥物管理局（FDA）的全面核准，但該機構已採取行動，提高藥物的取得管道。根據美國公共廣播電台報導，食品藥物管理局已擴大該藥物的取得權限，允許醫師在最終核准程序完成前，就能將藥物提供給患者使用。對於那些一直在等待新且有效治療選擇的患者來說，這是一個正面的訊號。

paragraphs.en: For many, this discovery offers a new sense of hope. Paula Hanford, the chief executive of the organization Pancreatic Cancer Action, stated that this is one of the most significant developments she has ever seen. As researchers continue to study the long-term effects of daraxonrasib, the medical community remains optimistic that this new approach will help turn a once-untreatable disease into one that can be managed more effectively.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多人而言，這項發現帶來了新的希望。胰臟癌行動組織（Pancreatic Cancer Action）執行長寶拉·漢福德（Paula Hanford）表示，這是她見過最重要的發展之一。隨著研究人員持續研究 daraxonrasib 的長期影響，醫學界仍保持樂觀，認為這種新療法將有助於將一種曾經無法治療的疾病，轉變為可以更有效控制的疾病。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An event, idea, or procedure that effects a significant shift in the current manner of doing or thinking about something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 改變遊戲規則的人事物，指帶來重大轉折的關鍵發展。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞由 game 和 changer 組成，形容徹底改變現狀的事物。文中：Experts at the conference described the findings as a "gamechanger" and one of the most important developments in the field in decades.
原為體育術語（如網球或棒球），這裡借用來形容研究成果非常成功。文中：Dr. Julie Gralow, the chief medical officer at ASCO, described the study as a "grand slam" for cancer research.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To change or develop into something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡的 turn 表示「轉變」某種狀態，而非物理上的旋轉。文中：the medical community remains optimistic that this new approach will help turn a once-untreatable disease into one that can be managed more effectively.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Daily pill can double survival time for world’s deadliest cancer, trial shows

sources.name: NPR Health
sources.title: Pancreatic cancer was once considered almost untreatable. A new drug is offering hope

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: 'A Grand Slam' in Pancreatic Cancer: Novel Pill Doubles Survival

quiz.question: According to the clinical trial results, what is the specific average survival time for patients who took daraxonrasib?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，臨床試驗中服用 daraxonrasib 的患者平均存活時間為 13.2 個月。選項 A 與 B 是文中提到的傳統化療存活時間（6.6 至 6.7 個月），屬於干擾項。選項 D 則是錯誤的加總數據，並非文中提及的平均存活期。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why is the FDA's decision to provide 'expanded access' to the drug significant?

options.A: It means the drug has already been proven to cure pancreatic cancer completely.
options.B: It indicates that the drug is now cheaper than traditional chemotherapy.
options.C: It suggests the medical community considers the drug's benefits to outweigh the risks even before final approval.
options.D: It confirms that the drug has no side effects for the majority of patients.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章最後兩段提到該藥物尚未獲得 FDA 正式批准，但 FDA 已採取擴大取得管道的措施。這暗示在緊急且缺乏治療選項的情況下，監管機構認為該藥物帶來的生存效益大於等待正式批准的時間成本。選項 A 過度誇大療效；選項 B 文中未提及價格；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中提到仍有副作用（如皮疹）。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the author mean by describing the Kras protein as 'fuel' for cancer cells?

options.A: It provides the energy and instructions necessary for cancer cells to multiply.
options.B: It is a substance that doctors inject into the body to burn away tumors.
options.C: It is a chemical that makes patients feel physically exhausted during treatment.
options.D: It is a rare protein that only appears in the final stages of the disease.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到 Kras 蛋白「acts like a fuel that tells cancer cells to grow and divide」，意指該蛋白質如同燃料般驅動癌細胞不斷複製與生長。選項 B 誤解了燃料的隱喻；選項 C 與文中提到的副作用無關；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中提到該蛋白存在於幾乎所有的胰臟癌細胞中，並非僅在末期出現。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the new drug, daraxonrasib?

options.A: It is a revolutionary treatment that has completely replaced chemotherapy for all cancer types.
options.B: It represents a promising advancement in pancreatic cancer treatment by improving survival rates and patient quality of life.
options.C: It is a controversial drug that has caused more side effects than traditional medical treatments.
options.D: It is the first drug in history to be approved by the FDA for treating digestive system cancers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。全文重點在於介紹 daraxonrasib 如何透過針對 Kras 蛋白，顯著延長胰臟癌患者的存活時間，並相較於傳統化療提供更好的生活品質，被醫學界視為重大突破。選項 A 誇大其詞；選項 C 與事實相反，文中強調其副作用較輕；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到該藥物尚未獲得正式批准，且未稱其為消化系統癌症的首例藥物。
